dev_cache_rules.rationale: pip HTTP and wheel cache entries are package artifacts that can be regenerated by future installs.
npm content-addressed cache entries are verified package artifacts that can be regenerated by npm.
Yarn package cache entries are downloaded dependencies that can be regenerated by Yarn.
pnpm store entries are dependency artifacts; unused content can be regenerated from lockfiles and registries.
NuGet global package cache entries are restore artifacts that can be regenerated by dotnet restore.
Cargo registry cache entries are crate download artifacts that can be regenerated by Cargo.
Go build cache entries are compiled artifacts that can be regenerated by the Go toolchain.
Go module cache entries are downloaded module artifacts that can be regenerated by the Go toolchain.

dev_cache_rules.official_cleanup_command: gradle --stop; remove selected caches after review
dev_cache_rules.rationale: Gradle cache entries are dependency and build artifacts that can be regenerated by Gradle builds.
Maven local repository artifacts can be regenerated from declared dependencies and remote repositories.
Poetry cache entries are downloaded package artifacts that can be regenerated from lockfiles and Python package indexes.
Pipenv cache entries are dependency resolution and download artifacts that can be regenerated from Pipfile and Pipfile.lock.
pre-commit cache entries are hook environments and repositories that can be recreated on the next hook run.

dev_cache_rules.official_cleanup_command: Remove reviewed node-gyp cache entries or let npm/node-gyp recreate headers on demand.
dev_cache_rules.rationale: node-gyp cache entries are downloaded Node.js headers and build artifacts that can be regenerated by native module builds.
uv cache entries are Python package wheels, source distributions, and metadata that can be regenerated by uv.

dev_cache_rules.official_cleanup_command: Use Gradle cleanup workflows or remove reviewed Android Gradle dependency cache entries after stopping Gradle daemons.
dev_cache_rules.rationale: Android Gradle plugin cache entries are dependency artifacts that can be regenerated by Gradle builds.

dev_cache_rules.official_cleanup_command: Use VS Code developer cache cleanup or remove reviewed CachedData entries while VS Code is closed.
dev_cache_rules.rationale: VS Code CachedData contains regenerated workbench and extension host bytecode cache, not user workspaces or settings.

dev_cache_rules.official_cleanup_command: Use JetBrains IDE cache invalidation workflows before removing reviewed index cache entries.
dev_cache_rules.rationale: JetBrains index cache entries are regenerated IDE metadata and do not include project source files.

package_cache_rules.official_cleanup_command: winget source reset --force or remove stale installer payloads only after review
package_cache_rules.rationale: WinGet package download payloads are installer artifacts that can be re-downloaded by winget.
Scoop cache entries are downloaded package archives that can be fetched again by Scoop.

package_cache_rules.official_cleanup_command: choco clean --yes or remove failed package payloads after review
package_cache_rules.rationale: Chocolatey lib-bad entries are failed or partial package artifacts that can be recreated by reinstall attempts.
Chocolatey cache entries are downloaded package payloads that can be fetched again by Chocolatey.
uv cache entries are Python package artifacts and wheels that can be regenerated by uv.

package_cache_rules.official_cleanup_command: Remove reviewed vcpkg downloads cache entries or use vcpkg cache cleanup workflows after review.
package_cache_rules.rationale: vcpkg downloads are source and binary package payloads that can be fetched again from package registries.

package_cache_rules.official_cleanup_command: pipx reinstall-all or remove reviewed pipx cache entries after review
package_cache_rules.rationale: pipx cache entries are package installation artifacts that can be regenerated from Python package indexes.

package_cache_rules.official_cleanup_command: Use Microsoft Store/App Installer maintenance workflows; review cache payloads before removal.
package_cache_rules.rationale: App Installer LocalCache entries are package delivery artifacts; Store app data and package registrations remain excluded.

package_cache_rules.official_cleanup_command: Prefer vendor uninstallers or Windows Installer repair workflows; review before removing cached installer payloads.
package_cache_rules.rationale: Package Cache can contain installer payloads; cleanwin keeps it review-first because it may affect repair or uninstall workflows.

package_cache_rules.official_cleanup_command: Use Visual Studio Installer cleanup workflows before removing reviewed package payloads.
package_cache_rules.rationale: Visual Studio package cache entries are installer payloads and must remain tied to official repair/update workflows.

browser_cache_rules.official_cleanup_command: Use Chrome > Clear browsing data and clear cached images/files only.
browser_cache_rules.rationale: Chrome Cache directory stores temporary web resources separate from Cookies, Login Data, and profile databases.

browser_cache_rules.official_cleanup_command: Use Edge > Clear browsing data and clear cached images/files only.
browser_cache_rules.rationale: Edge Code Cache stores regenerated script cache data and does not contain the primary cookie/session database.

browser_cache_rules.rationale: Firefox cache2 stores temporary browser cache entries that can be rebuilt from future browsing.
